claudeExecutionRequest.mission: Apply security fixes directly in the codebase for the confirmed issues below. Prefer minimal, production-safe changes that preserve existing behavior where possible. Complete code changes, any required schema or env changes, and regression tests in one pass.

workingRules.rule: Do not trust client-provided shop identity unless it is cryptographically verified or bound to trusted server-side session state.
Fail closed on authentication and authorization checks.
Preserve tenant isolation for every read, write, and Shopify Admin API action.
Do not introduce placeholder security checks that only decode data without verifying authenticity.
Prefer actionable tests that prove the vulnerability is closed.

priorityOrder.item: Protect Shopify offline tokens at rest

task.title: Remove shop impersonation paths and enforce real Shopify identity verification
task.problem: The code in app/utils/auth.ts currently treats a decoded bearer token as trusted even though the JWT signature is not verified, and it also accepts x-shopify-shop-domain as fallback identity. Server actions rely on this helper for tenant scoping and privileged operations.

requiredChanges.change: Replace the custom token parsing path with Shopify-supported session-token verification, or another server-trusted session validation mechanism already available in the stack.
Remove acceptance of x-shopify-shop-domain as an authentication fallback unless it is validated against trusted server-owned session state.
Ensure requireVerifiedShop() fails closed when verification fails.
Review all call sites of requireVerifiedShop() and keep their semantics unchanged except for rejecting unverified callers.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Forged bearer tokens with modified payloads are rejected.
Requests that only provide x-shopify-shop-domain are rejected.
Tenant-scoped server actions only execute when shop identity comes from verified Shopify auth.

tests.test: Add regression tests for forged JWT payloads.
Add regression tests for spoofed x-shopify-shop-domain headers.

task.title: Stop exposing unauthenticated arbitrary URL audits as an open browser-execution service
task.problem: app/actions/audit.ts exports runAccessibilityAudit(url), which launches Playwright for arbitrary user-supplied HTTPS URLs. The client calls this path directly from app/components/AuditTab.tsx without authentication or robust abuse controls.

requiredChanges.change: Require authenticated tenancy for expensive browser-based audits, or remove the arbitrary URL audit path if it is not required for product behavior.
If any public path remains, apply strict abuse controls that do not rely solely on untrusted shop values.
Add a clear boundary between auditing the verified tenant store and auditing arbitrary external URLs.
Keep the existing verified-shop audit flow working.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Unauthenticated users cannot launch unlimited Chromium audits against arbitrary HTTPS targets.
The verified-shop audit flow still works for legitimate embedded users.

tests.test: Add coverage proving the arbitrary unauthenticated path is blocked or no longer reachable.

task.title: Protect Shopify offline access tokens at rest
task.problem: Shopify offline tokens are stored as plaintext in the shopify_sessions table and are read back directly for Shopify Admin API client construction.

requiredChanges.change: Encrypt access tokens before persistence using a server-side secret not exposed to the client.
Decrypt tokens only at the point where a Shopify client is constructed on the server.
Add an environment variable contract for the encryption key and fail safely when it is missing.
Provide a migration path for existing plaintext rows, either via backward-compatible reads plus re-save, or an explicit migration strategy documented in code comments or setup docs.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Newly stored database rows do not contain usable plaintext Shopify tokens.
Existing application behavior still works when retrieving sessions for Shopify API calls.

tests.test: Add focused tests for encrypt/decrypt helpers or equivalent session-storage logic.

implementationNotes.note: Prefer fixing authentication first because the other server actions depend on that trust boundary.
Keep edits small and localized where possible.
If the repo lacks an existing test harness, add the lightest practical tests or a documented verification script consistent with the project stack.
Do not silently weaken existing SSRF checks while fixing audit access controls.

expectedDeliverables.deliverable: Updated application code implementing the fixes.
Any needed schema or migration changes.
Regression tests or equivalent automated verification for each fixed issue.
A short summary of what changed, any new environment variables, and any manual migration steps required.
